rulings.Case Title: RONALD STORDAHL VS. AMCORD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Although unclear from defendant's moving papers, assuming defendant is attempting to sustain its initial burden by demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by submitting admissible evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibits H and I and to the documents attached to the Yi declaration are sustained. However even if that evidence is considered, it does not negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Specifically, it does not demonstrate that the Kaiser gun plastic cement to which plaintiff was exposed did not contain asbestos. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is off calendar as plaintiff has dismissed such claim against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD STORDAHL VS. AMCORD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony demonstrates plaintiff has knowledge that he was exposed to Kaiser Gypsum products. Defendant's Separate Statement did not successfully demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Kaiser Gypsum products to which he was exposed contained asbestos. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of submitting admissible evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff's discovery responses show that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibits I, J, K and L are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH RHODES VS. BERGLUND FAMILY VINEYARDS, A CALIFORNIA LIMITES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Yaeger and Kirk's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Specifically, defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of whether plaintiff was exposed to any dust from a product supplied by/purchased from Yaeger and Kirk. Defendant concedes that plaintiff has evidence that her father and/or brother purchased Kaiser Gypsum and/or Georgia Pacific joint compound from defendant. Defendant does not argue that plaintiff does not possess and cannot obtain evidence that such joint compound(s) contained asbestos and, even if defendant had made that an issue, plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories would preclude defendant from sustaining its burden on that topic. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration, the deposition testimony of Daniel Tinney and the declaration of John Templin create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Donald Yaeger are overruled, however Mr. Yaeger's declaration is not sufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled and its objections to the interrogatory responses of Kaiser Gypsum and Georgia Pacific are sustained. Defendant's objections to John Templin's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 15 (the 5th and 6th bullet points only), 35, 37, 38 and 39 and to Exhibits F and G attached thereto and are overruled as to all other objections. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
